intended_use: EpiMonitor is a prescription only medical device system composed of a wearable device "EmbracePlus" and paired mobile software application "EpiMonitor" intended as an adjunct to seizure monitoring in
intended_use_full: EpiMonitor is a prescription only medical device system composed of a wearable device "EmbracePlus" and paired mobile software application "EpiMonitor" intended as an adjunct to seizure monitoring in adults and children aged 6 and up in a home environment or healthcare facilities. The device is worn on the wrist and senses Electrodermal Activity (EDA) and motion data to detect patterns that may be associated with either primary or secondary generalized tonic clonic seizures in patients with epilepsy or at risk of having epilepsy. When a seizure event is detected, the wearable device component of EpiMonitor sends a command to a paired mobile device where the EpiMonitor App is programmed to initiate an alert to a designated caregiver. The EpiMonitor app incorporates additional detection sensitivity modes, "high" for use during periods of rest or sleeping or "low" for use during periods of low-intensity activity, in order to reduce false alarm incidents. EpiMonitor records, stores and transmits accelerometer, EDA, peripheral skin temperature and activity data for subsequent retrospective review by a trained healthcare professional via a cloud-based software.
target_condition: Generalized tonic-clonic seizures (primary or secondary) in patients with epilepsy or at risk of epilepsy
ground_truth: Adjudicated tonic-clonic seizure data collected from patients observed in Epilepsy Monitoring Units (gold standard video-EEG methodology); real-world data captured using Embrace2 wearable device
other_metrics: Positive Percentage Agreement (PPA) for Low-Sensitivity Mode: 0.895 (6-21 years, EMU data), 1.000 (>21 years, EMU data), 0.87 (6-21 years, real-world data), 0.8 (>21 years, real-world data). False Alarm Rate (FAR) per 24 hours for Low-Sensitivity Mode: 0.70 (6-21 years, EMU data), 0.28 (>21 years, EMU data), 0.34 (6-21 years, real-world data), 0.25 (>21 years, real-world data).
